evidence.claim: ENERGY STAR washers use ~20% less energy and ~33% less water than minimum-efficiency models; lifetime savings average $370-$520.
Front-load washers have lower operating costs but require door-seal maintenance (mold risk) and longer cycle times than top-load.

confabulationPatterns.reality: Cleaning performance depends on drum design, wash action, and detergent dosing — not on maximalist 'power' claims. CR and Wirecutter have measured actual cleaning scores.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Is the cleaning claim backed by an independent score (CR, Wirecutter)?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Not standardized. Actual cu.ft. number matters; 4.5 cu.ft. is 'large'; 5.0+ is 'extra-large'; 6.0+ is 'mega'. Cross-check the cu.ft. figure.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: What is the actual cu.ft. capacity?

compatibilityQuestions: Is your laundry-room floor reinforced? High-spin front-load washers (1200+ RPM) can damage weaker floors.
Stackable needed for apartment / condo? Limits the model set.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Extended warranty (often poor value; parts failure <5% in first 3 years)
Door-seal cleaning for front-load (mold prevention) or pedestal
